evidence_quotes.quote: video game industry has been maliciously destroying (making the game inaccessible to play for consumers) games after they no longer deem them profitable, with no EOL (end of life) plans for consumers to still be able to play the games they bought

paragraphs.text: Hello, I'm here to comment on this new Digital Fairness Act to request that you consider adding an additional protection for video game consumers. For the past 15 years, the video game industry has been maliciously destroying (making the game inaccessible to play for consumers) games after they no longer deem them profitable, with no EOL (end of life) plans for consumers to still be able to play the games they bought, effectively robbing them of the product that they paid for. This has happened to me personally with the game 'Little Big Planet', where Sony gave no means of saving UGC (user generated content) after shutting down the servers permanently with no warning following the game's servers being hacked hack. Literally tens of millions of user creations that were advertised in the game's marketing and box art have now been wiped forever with no way to preserve them, outside of video recordings. The industry has been running a smear campaign on this effort, claiming it is too expensive and costly to implement EOL in their games. This has been proven to be a lie, as implementing ways to give the consumers a means of still accessing the game they paid for have never been easier, including allowing players to create their own servers to host games, and including development tools so the players can maintain the game they paid for after the developers and publishers have moved on from it. This will also help preserve games as an art form, allowing future generations to still be able to experience the game long after it was abandoned. Please look at stopkillinggames.com for more information about this, and how you can help protect the consumer rights of gamers in the EU. This is an incredibly important issue with millions of EU citizens, as the Stop Killing Games European Citizens Initiative petition has just surpassed 1.4 million signatures. https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home Thank you for reading!
